items.title: Peter Thiel’s big bet on solar-powered cow collars
items.summary: Why did Founders Fund invest $220 million in cattle management startup Halter?

items.title: Embattled startup Delve has ‘parted ways’ with Y Combinator
items.summary: The controversy around Delve appears to have cost the compliance startup its relationship with accelerator Y Combinator.

items.title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
items.summary: It’s about to become more expensive for Claude Code subscribers to use Anthropic’s coding assistant with OpenClaw and other third-party tools.

items.title: After fighting malware for decades, this cybersecurity veteran is now hacking drones
items.summary: Mikko Hyppönen is one of the most recognizable faces of the cybersecurity industry. After fighting computer viruses, worms, and malware, for more than 35 years, he tells TechCrunch why he is now working on systems to stop killer drones.

items.title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
items.summary: Glen Anderson, president of Rainmaker Securities, says the secondary market for private shares has never been more active — with Anthropic the hottest trade around, OpenAI losing ground, and SpaceX's looming IPO poised to reshape the landscape for everyone.

items.title: Lucid blames dip in Q1 sales on seat supplier issue
items.summary: Lucid said it has resolved the problem and is not changing its guidance for 2026.

items.title: The anonymous social app that thinks it can work in Saudi Arabia
items.summary: When Fizz quietly debuted in Saudi Arabia, founder and CEO Teddy Solomon wasn’t expecting the app to catch on like it did.

items.title: Tesla’s Texas factory workforce reportedly shrunk 22% in 2025
items.summary: Tesla's headcount fell from 21,191 workers to 16,506 workers in 2025, according to a report, as it grappled with its second straight year of declining sales.

items.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
items.summary: In addition to Lightcap's new role, OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch will be stepping away from the company to focus on cancer recovery, with a plan to return when her health allows.

items.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
items.summary: Anthropic has purchased the stealth biotech AI startup Coefficient Bio in a $400 million stock deal, according to The Information and Eric Newcomer.

items.title: Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC
items.summary: With the midterms right around the corner, the new group is positioned to back candidates who support the AI company's policy agenda.

items.title: Best iPad apps to boost productivity and make your life easier
items.summary: There are many iPad apps to help you organize recipes, sync tasks across devices, be more productive, and manage your notes.

items.title: AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
items.summary: Meta, Microsoft, and Google are all betting big on new natural gas power plants to run their AI data centers. They may regret it.

items.title: People would rather have an Amazon warehouse in their backyard than a data center
items.summary: A new poll shows that the debate over data centers is far from settled.

items.title: The final days of the Tesla Model X and S are here. All bets are on the Cybercab.
items.summary: It's a new era for Tesla — and one that will rely on its ability to launch the Cybercab and mass-produce the Optimus robot.

items.title: Europe’s cyber agency blames hacking gangs for massive data breach and leak
items.summary: CERT-EU blamed the cybercrime group TeamPCP for the recent hack on the European Commission, and said the notorious ShinyHunters gang was responsible for leaking the stolen data online.

items.title: The Facebook insider building content moderation for the AI era
items.summary: Moonbounce has raised $12 million to grow its AI control engine that converts content moderation policies into consistent, predictable AI behavior.

items.title: Amazon hits sellers with ‘fuel surcharge’ as Iran war roils global energy markets
items.summary: The e-commerce giant called the surcharge "temporary" but couldn't give a date for when the policy would be retired.

items.title: Telehealth giant Hims & Hers says its customer support system was hacked
items.summary: The U.S. telehealth giant says hackers stole customer support ticket data over the course of several days in February.

items.title: Artemis II is NASA’s last moon mission without Silicon Valley
items.summary: Next time around, the pressure will be on SpaceX and Blue Origin.
